name: Zeebrugge
summary: Zeebrugge is a busy port on Belgium's North Sea coast and the gateway to Bruges, a fairy-tale medieval town just 15 kilometres away with cobbled lanes, pretty canals and a giant brick belfry tower.
funFacts: The Belfry of Bruges is 83 metres tall, leans slightly to one side, and you have to climb 366 steep stone steps to reach the top.
The whole old town of Bruges is so well preserved that UNESCO made it a World Heritage Site in the year 2000.
Bruges is nicknamed the 'Venice of the North' because little canals weave between its houses, with old stone bridges arching over them.
Belgians eat about 5.6 kilograms of chocolate per person every year, and many famous chocolate shops fill the streets of Bruges.
Tiny grey shrimp from the North Sea off Zeebrugge are still caught by horses pulling nets through the shallow surf, a 700-year-old tradition.
thingsToDo: Climb all 366 steep steps of the Belfry of Bruges for a giant view across red rooftops and canals.
Glide through Bruges on a half-hour canal boat ride past swans, towers and centuries-old houses.
Wander Markt square in Bruges and watch horse-drawn carriages clip-clop past colourful gabled buildings.
Visit the quiet Beguinage, a walled garden of little white houses where nuns once lived among trees.
Build sandcastles on Zeebrugge's wide North Sea beach, right beside the harbour wall.
Sample real Belgian chocolate, then grab a paper cone of crispy frites with mayo from a friterie.

wildlife.name: Harbour Seal
wildlife.description: Plump grey seal often spotted resting on Zeebrugge's harbour wall and sandy beaches.

wildlife.name: Eurasian Oystercatcher
wildlife.description: Black-and-white seabird with a long bright orange beak for prising open shellfish.

wildlife.name: Common Starfish
wildlife.description: Five-armed orange sea star found in shallow rock pools along the North Sea coast.

wildlife.name: Brown Shrimp
wildlife.description: Tiny grey shrimp scooped from the surf and eaten by Belgians as a salty snack.

localWord.meaning: Good day, in Dutch (the language people speak in Bruges and Zeebrugge).

images.alt: The tall brick Belfry tower of Bruges rising above the medieval Markt square.
Bruges' Markt square seen from above, lined with colourful gabled buildings.
A pretty canal in Bruges with old brick houses and the Belfry tower behind them.
The tall brick spire of the Church of Our Lady in Bruges seen from a canal bridge.
Whitewashed houses of the Beguinage in Bruges set around a leafy green courtyard.
Wide sandy North Sea beach at Zeebrugge with the seafront promenade alongside.
A speckled grey harbour seal floating in the cold North Atlantic water.
A black-and-white Eurasian oystercatcher with a long bright orange beak.
An orange common starfish with five chunky arms from the Belgian North Sea.
A tiny grey-brown shrimp from the North Sea, the kind Belgians love to eat.
